detail.title: Maximum Hamming Distances
hints: For each nums[i], complement it (for each bit, if it is 1, it becomes 0 and vice-versa).
Instead of finding the maximum Hamming distance from x = nums[i], let's think of finding the minimum Hamming distance from the complement of x to any element of the array.
Create a graph with V = {0, 1, ..., 2m - 1}. Put an edge between two vertices if they differ in exactly one bit.
Run a multi-source BFS from elements of nums.
Now for each x, to find its minimum Hamming distance from elements of the array, simply calculate its shortest path from array elements.
